metadata.notes: Content overhaul to Wingspan standard — Rogue B, March 2026. Sources: official Stonemaier rulebook, BGG FAQ, r/boardgames strategy threads.

key_rulings.text: On your turn, you must take exactly 1 of 3 actions: Move, Seek, or Make.
When your seeker enters a hex with resources, you automatically pick up all resources on that hex.
If the attacker and defender bid the same number of pies, the defender wins.
The attacker immediately loses 1 friendship when initiating a pie fight, before pies are revealed.
Once a trophy is earned, it cannot be lost, even if the condition that earned it is no longer met.
When a player places their 4th trophy, finish the current round so all players have had an equal number of turns.
When you Seek, you may place each rolled resource on any hex on the board — they do not need to be adjacent to your seekers.
To upgrade, spend 2 matching resources (2 apples OR 2 gems) from your seekers.
To craft a spell, spend 1 apple and 1 gem. Draw 2 spell cards, keep 1, return the other to the bottom of the deck.
When a seeker enters Castle Everfree, all resources that seeker carries must be deposited into the castle.

subtopics.title: Components
subtopics.content:
#### Board & Tiles
- **1 game board** — A hex-grid map of the Kingdom of Pomme. Castle Everfree sits at the center. Eight territories surround it, with base camp spaces around the outer edge.
- **8 base camp tiles** — One for each animal kingdom. These mark where each player's seekers start and retreat to after losing a pie fight.
- **4 power-up tiles per player** (32 total) — Small double-sided tiles. One side shows the basic ability, the other the upgraded ability. Each player gets 2 Move power-ups and 2 Make power-ups.

#### Miniatures & Tokens
- **16 seeker miniatures** — 2 per animal kingdom. The 8 kingdoms are: foxes, bears, bunnies, owls, turtles, mice, pandas, and cats. Each pair shares a player color.
- **8 trophy tokens** — Flat tokens in each player's color. Place these on the trophy track printed on the board when you earn trophies.
- **24 pie tokens** — Small circular tokens. Store these behind your player screen. Used for pie fights and some quest completions.
- **8 friendship tokens** — One per player, placed on the Friendship Track printed on the board.

#### Resources
- **20 apple tokens** — Red octagonal tokens representing one of the two resource types.
- **20 gem tokens** — Blue diamond-shaped tokens representing the other resource type.

#### Cards & Dice
- **24 quest cards** — Each shows two options: a generous choice and a greedy choice.
- **16 magic spell cards** — One-time-use ability cards kept hidden behind your screen.
- **4 story dice** — Custom dice with faces showing apple, gem, and quest symbols.
- **8 player screens** — Cardboard screens to hide your pie tokens and spell cards.

#### Other
- **1 first-player token** — A pie-shaped marker.
- **1 Automountie board + tokens** — For solo play.
- **1 rulebook**

subtopics.title: Game Structure & Setup
subtopics.content:
#### How the Game Works
My Little Scythe is a race to earn **4 trophies**. There are 8 different trophy categories, and you only need 4 of them. On each turn, you take one of three actions — Move, Seek, or Make — to explore the Kingdom of Pomme, collect resources, deliver them to Castle Everfree, complete quests, and build up your abilities.

#### Setting Up the Board
1. **Unfold the game board** and place it in the center of the table. You'll see the hex grid with Castle Everfree in the middle, 8 territories around it, and base camp spots on the outer edge.
2. **Each player chooses an animal kingdom.** Take the 2 matching seeker miniatures, 1 trophy token, 1 friendship token, 4 power-up tiles, and 1 player screen.
3. **Place your 2 seekers** on your base camp space at the edge of the board.
4. **Place your friendship token** on space 3 of the Friendship Track. Everyone starts at friendship level 3.
5. **Set your 4 power-up tiles** in front of you, basic side face-up.
6. **Take 3 pie tokens** from the supply and place them behind your screen.
7. **Shuffle the quest cards** and place the deck face-down near the board.
8. **Shuffle the magic spell cards** and place that deck face-down nearby.
9. **Place all apple and gem tokens** in a central supply within reach of all players.
10. **Give the first-player token** to the youngest player (or choose randomly).

#### Checkpoint
Each player should now have: 2 seekers on their base camp, 1 friendship token on space 3, 4 power-up tiles (basic side up), 3 pies behind their screen, and a player screen. The apple/gem supply, quest deck, and spell deck should be within reach of everyone.

subtopics.title: Turn Flow
subtopics.content:
#### Your Turn
On your turn, you perform exactly **one** of three actions: **Move**, **Seek**, or **Make**. After your action, check if you've earned any new trophies. Then play passes clockwise.

#### Action 1: Move
Move **one or both** of your seekers a total number of hexes up to your movement allowance (starts at 2, upgradeable to 3). You split movement freely between your seekers — for example, move one seeker 2 hexes and the other 0, or each 1 hex.

**Picking up resources:** Your seekers automatically pick up all apples and gems from every hex they enter or pass through. Resources ride along with the seeker miniature — stack them next to it or on your player mat.

**Delivering to Castle Everfree:** When a seeker carrying resources enters the Castle Everfree hex at the center of the board, you **must** deposit all resources that seeker is carrying into the castle. Each apple delivered counts toward the Apple Delivery trophy. Each gem counts toward the Gem Delivery trophy. You also gain **+1 friendship** for each delivery trip (not per resource).

**Entering an occupied hex:** If your seeker enters a hex occupied by an opponent's seeker, two things can happen:
- If you pick up resources from that hex, you lose **1 friendship**.
- You may choose to initiate a **pie fight** (see Pie Fights below). Attacking costs **1 friendship**.

#### Action 2: Seek
Roll all **4 story dice**. Look at the results:
- For each **apple** face: take 1 apple token from the supply and place it on **any hex** on the board.
- For each **gem** face: take 1 gem token from the supply and place it on **any hex** on the board.
- For each **quest** symbol: draw 1 quest card from the deck and keep it face-down behind your screen.

You choose where to place each resource — they don't have to go near your seekers. You can stack multiple resources on the same hex or spread them across different hexes.

If your Seek power-up is upgraded, you may place **1 additional resource** of your choice (apple or gem) from the supply onto any hex after rolling.

#### Action 3: Make
Spend resources currently carried by your seekers to do **one** of the following:

- **Upgrade** (cost: 2 apples OR 2 gems) — Flip one of your 4 power-up tiles to its upgraded side. This permanently enhances that action for the rest of the game. Your first upgrade earns the **Upgrade trophy**.
- **Craft a Magic Spell** (cost: 1 apple + 1 gem) — Draw 2 spell cards, keep 1, return the other to the bottom of the deck. Spells are one-time abilities you can play on a future turn. Keep spells hidden behind your screen.
- **Bake Pies** (cost: 1 apple OR 1 gem per pie) — Convert resources into pie tokens. Store pies behind your screen. Accumulating **3 pies at once** earns the **Pie trophy**.

subtopics.title: Pie Fights, Quests & Friendship
subtopics.content:
#### Pie Fights (Combat)
A pie fight happens when your seeker moves into a hex occupied by an opponent's seeker and you choose to fight.

1. **The attacker loses 1 friendship immediately** — before anything else happens.
2. Both players **secretly choose** how many pie tokens to bid (minimum 0). Place your bid behind your screen.
3. Both players **reveal simultaneously**.
4. **Highest bid wins.** Ties go to the **defender**.
5. **All bid pies are spent** — both the winner's and loser's pies go back to the supply.
6. The **loser's seeker** retreats to their base camp. Any resources that seeker was carrying are **dropped on the battle hex**.
7. The **winner** earns the **Combat trophy** (if not already earned) and may pick up any resources on the hex.

**Key detail:** You can bid 0 pies. If the attacker bids 0 and the defender bids 0, the defender wins (ties favor defenders).

#### Quests
To complete a quest, you need:
- At least **1 quest card** behind your screen.
- At least **1 seeker** on a **quest hex** (hexes marked with a scroll icon on the board).

Reveal your quest card. Each quest presents **two options**:
- **Generous choice** — Smaller reward, but you gain friendship.
- **Greedy choice** — Larger reward, but you lose friendship.

Quest rewards include pies, resources, movement, spells, or friendship boosts. Completing any quest earns the **Quest trophy** (if not already earned). Discard the quest card after resolving.

#### The Friendship Track
Friendship ranges from **0 to 8**. Everyone starts at **3**.

**Friendship goes up when you:**
- Deliver resources to Castle Everfree (+1 per trip)
- Choose the generous option on a quest
- Use certain spell effects

**Friendship goes down when you:**
- Attack another player in a pie fight (-1)
- Pick up resources from a hex where an opponent's seeker is present (-1)
- Choose the greedy quest option

Reaching friendship **level 6** earns the **Friendship trophy**. Friendship also serves as the primary **tiebreaker** at game end.

subtopics.title: Trophies & Endgame
subtopics.content:
#### The 8 Trophy Categories
You need **4 trophies** to trigger the endgame. Each can be earned once per player.

1. **Quest Trophy** — Complete at least 1 quest card.
2. **Combat Trophy** — Win at least 1 pie fight.
3. **Apple Delivery Trophy** — Deliver a total of 3 apples to Castle Everfree (cumulative across the game).
4. **Gem Delivery Trophy** — Deliver a total of 3 gems to Castle Everfree (cumulative across the game).
5. **Upgrade Trophy** — Perform at least 1 upgrade (flip a power-up tile).
6. **Pie Trophy** — Have 3 or more pie tokens at one time.
7. **Friendship Trophy** — Reach level 6 on the Friendship Track.
8. **Spell Trophy** — Craft and use at least 2 magic spells during the game.

**Trophies are permanent.** If you earn the Pie trophy by reaching 3 pies and then spend pies in combat, you keep the trophy. Same for friendship — if you earn it at level 6 and later drop below 6, the trophy stays.

#### Endgame
When any player places their **4th trophy** on the trophy track, the endgame triggers. Continue play until **all players have taken an equal number of turns** (finish the current round).

#### Winning
1. The player with the **most trophies** wins.
2. Tie on trophies? The player with **higher friendship** wins.
3. Still tied? The player with **more pie tokens** wins.
4. Still tied? **Shared victory**.

#### Power-Up Tiles Reference
Each player has 4 power-up tiles (2 Move, 2 Make). Here's what they do when upgraded:
- **Move +1** — Increases total movement from 2 hexes to 3 hexes.
- **Safe Passage** — Move through hexes with opponent seekers without triggering combat.
- **Efficient Upgrade** — Reduce upgrade cost by 1 resource (from 2 to 1).
- **Extra Pie** — Gain 1 extra pie when baking pies.

#### Solo Mode (Automountie)
The Automountie is an AI opponent. On its turn, draw an action token to determine Move, Seek, or Make. It follows a priority flowchart for movement — heading to Castle Everfree with resources, or toward the nearest resources. You must reach 4 trophies before the Automountie does.

subtopics.title: Beginner Strategy
subtopics.content:
#### Opening Moves (Turns 1-3)
- **Turn 1: Seek.** Rolling dice and placing resources near your base camp is almost always the best opening. Place apples or gems 1-2 hexes from your seekers for easy pickup next turn.
- **Turn 2: Move.** Send your seekers to collect the resources you just placed. If possible, head toward Castle Everfree to start working on delivery trophies.
- **Turn 3: Deliver or Seek again.** If your seeker is near Castle Everfree with resources, move in and deliver. Otherwise, Seek again to seed more resources.

#### The Fastest Path to 4 Trophies
The most reliable strategy hits these 4 trophies:
1. **Apple Delivery** — Deliver 3 apples to Castle Everfree.
2. **Upgrade** — Spend 2 matching resources to flip a power-up tile. The movement upgrade (2→3 hexes) is almost always the best first upgrade.
3. **Friendship** — Reach level 6. Start at 3, gain +1 per Castle delivery, and choose generous quest options. Three deliveries + one generous quest gets you there.
4. **Quest or Gem Delivery** — Whichever you encounter first.

#### When to Fight
Combat earns a trophy but costs 1 friendship and however many pies you bid. The ideal time to fight is:
- When you're at **friendship 5 or higher** (so losing 1 friendship doesn't hurt your Friendship trophy path).
- When your opponent is **carrying resources** you want — if you win, you get their dropped resources.
- When you have **3+ pies** and your opponent likely has fewer.

Avoid fighting at friendship 3 or below. Every point of friendship you lose is another delivery you need to make.

#### Resource Placement on Seek
- Place resources **near your own seekers** for easy pickup.
- Place on **quest hexes** if you have quest cards to complete.
- Place **far from opponents** to deny them easy resources.
- In a 2-player game, consider placing resources on your opponent's side as bait to draw them away from Castle Everfree.

#### Common Mistakes
- **Attacking too early.** Losing friendship at the start of the game means you need 4+ deliveries to reach the Friendship trophy instead of 3. Avoid fights until friendship 5+.
- **Hoarding resources on seekers.** Resources on your seekers are vulnerable to pie fights. Deliver them promptly.
- **Ignoring quests.** Quest cards are essentially free trophies — just get a seeker to a quest hex. The generous option gives friendship, which is often more valuable than the greedy option's larger reward.
- **Spending all pies in one fight.** Never bid everything. Keep at least 1 pie as a deterrent. Your opponent doesn't know how many pies you have.
- **Only chasing one trophy type.** You need 4 different trophies. Delivery + upgrade + friendship + quest is more efficient than trying to win 4 separate pie fights.

#### Upgrade Priority
1. **Movement +1** (2→3 hexes) — Best first upgrade. Faster deliveries, better map control, and harder for opponents to predict your reach.
2. **Extra Pie on Bake** — Strong second if you plan to pursue Combat or Pie trophies.
3. **Efficient Upgrade** — Only useful if you plan multiple upgrades (rare).
4. **Safe Passage** — Situational. Good in high-player-count games where the map is crowded.

subtopics.title: First-Game Walkthrough
subtopics.content:
#### Your First Game of My Little Scythe

**After Setup**: Each player has 2 seekers at their base camp, 3 pies behind their screen, friendship at 3, and 4 basic power-up tiles. The board is empty of resources. Your goal: earn 4 trophies.

**Turn 1 — Seek**: Take the 4 story dice and roll them. Say you roll 2 apples, 1 gem, and 1 quest. Take 2 apple tokens and 1 gem token from the supply. Place them on hexes near your base camp — ideally 1-2 hexes away from your seekers. Draw 1 quest card and tuck it behind your screen.

**Turn 2 — Move**: Move both seekers toward the resources you placed. Pick up everything they pass through. If one seeker collects 2 apples, great — that's enough for an upgrade or 2 deliveries toward the Apple Delivery trophy.

**Turn 3 — Make (Upgrade)**: If a seeker is carrying 2 apples or 2 gems, spend them to flip a power-up tile. Flip the Movement +1 tile — you now move 3 hexes instead of 2. You earn the **Upgrade trophy**. That's 1 of 4.

**Turns 4-5 — Seek and Move**: Seek to place more resources near your path to Castle Everfree. Move your seekers toward the castle, picking up resources along the way.

**Turn 6 — Move into Castle Everfree**: Move a seeker carrying apples into the center hex. Deposit all carried resources. If you delivered 3+ apples total, you earn the **Apple Delivery trophy** and gain +1 friendship (now at 4). That's 2 of 4.

**Turns 7-8 — Deliver more + Quest**: Keep delivering to gain friendship. If you're at a quest hex with a quest card, complete it — choose the generous option for +1 friendship. Earn the **Quest trophy** (3 of 4) and climb toward friendship 6.

**The Home Stretch**: With 3 trophies, you need 1 more. If your friendship is at 5, one more delivery gets you to 6 for the **Friendship trophy**. That's your 4th — the endgame triggers!

**Final Round**: All other players get to finish the round. Then count trophies. Most trophies wins. Tied? Highest friendship breaks it.
